[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Mervin+Manufacturing%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Mervin+Manufacturing%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Mervin+Manufacturing%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Mervin+Manufacturing%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(July 2025)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

is an American [snowboard](/wiki/Snowboard "Snowboard") manufacturer. Mervin is the parent company of [Lib Technologies](/wiki/Lib_Technologies "Lib Technologies") (aka Lib Tech), [GNU Snowboards](/w/index.php?title=GNU_Snowboards&action=edit&redlink=1 "GNU Snowboards (page does not exist)"), Roxy Snowboards, and Bent Metal bindings.[[1]](#cite_note-Times-2024-1) In the mid 1990s Mervin was also an OEM supplier for companies such as [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada")'s Luxury brand and for [The Movement Snowboards](/w/index.php?title=The_Movement_Snowboards&action=edit&redlink=1 "The Movement Snowboards (page does not exist)").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American snowboard manufacturer

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Mervin_Manufacturing "Special:EditPage/Mervin Manufacturing") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Mervin Manufacturing"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Mervin+Manufacturing%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Mervin+Manufacturing%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Mervin+Manufacturing%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Mervin+Manufacturing%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Mervin+Manufacturing%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Mervin+Manufacturing%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(July 2025)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

**Mervin Manufacturing** is an American [snowboard](/wiki/Snowboard "Snowboard") manufacturer. Mervin is the parent company of [Lib Technologies](/wiki/Lib_Technologies "Lib Technologies") (aka Lib Tech), [GNU Snowboards](/w/index.php?title=GNU_Snowboards&action=edit&redlink=1 "GNU Snowboards (page does not exist)"), Roxy Snowboards, and Bent Metal bindings.[[1]](#cite_note-Times-2024-1) In the mid 1990s Mervin was also an OEM supplier for companies such as [Canada](/wiki/Canada "Canada")'s Luxury brand and for [The Movement Snowboards](/w/index.php?title=The_Movement_Snowboards&action=edit&redlink=1 "The Movement Snowboards (page does not exist)").

Mervin was purchased by [Quiksilver](/wiki/Quiksilver "Quiksilver") in 1997 and later sold to Altamont Capital in 2013. It manufactures its snowboards in [Sequim, Washington](/wiki/Sequim,_Washington "Sequim, Washington").[[1]](#cite_note-Times-2024-1)
